Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins

Source: vignettes/workflows.Rmd
workflows.Rmd

This vignette walks through each admin workflow jinx supports. For setup and architecture details, see vignette("getting-started").

Onboarding a new team member

Via issue comment:

/jinx invite @username to website

What happens:

  1. gt_invite() sends an org invitation to the user
  2. A tracking issue is created with the team-specific onboarding checklist
  3. Once the user accepts, gt_finalize_onboarding() adds them to the appropriate team(s) and repos
  4. The tracking issue is closed

Programmatically:

gt_invite("octocat", "website")

Checking pending invitations:

gt_check_invitations()

Offboarding a team member

Via issue comment:

/jinx offboard @username from blog

What happens:

  1. gt_create_offboarding() opens an offboarding tracking issue
  2. The issue lists cleanup steps (remove from team, repos, external services)
  3. Once complete, gt_finalize_offboarding() removes org access and closes the issue

Programmatically:

gt_create_offboarding("octocat", "blog")

Setting up a new chapter

Via issue comment:

/jinx chapter-setup Berlin Germany

What happens:

  1. create_chapter_setup() opens a setup tracking issue
  2. The issue contains a checklist: create repos, add organizers, configure Meetup, set up social media
  3. create_chapter() generates the chapter JSON for the directory
  4. create_chapter_json_pr() opens a PR to add the chapter to the directory

Updating an existing chapter:

/jinx chapter-update Berlin Germany

Monitoring chapter health

Scheduled workflow runs periodically via check_chapter_health().

Via issue comment:

/jinx chapter-health

What happens:

  1. Reads chapter activity data (commits, issues, events)
  2. Classifies chapters as active, quiet, or inactive
  3. Posts a summary with recommendations

Outreach to inactive chapters:

statuses <- monitor_chapter_status()
prepare_inactivity_emails(statuses)

Blog post workflow

Adding a new blog entry

Via issue comment:

/jinx blog-add https://example.com/my-rladies-post

What happens:

  1. create_blog_entry() fetches metadata from the URL
  2. Creates a YAML entry and opens a PR to the blog repo
  3. post_blog_checklist() adds a review checklist to the PR

Checking blog links 

/jinx blog-check-links

Runs check_blog_links() to find broken URLs across all blog entries.

Auto-merge on publish date

auto_merge_pending() runs on a schedule and merges blog PRs whose publish date has arrived. This is triggered by the website-merge-pending.yml workflow.

Announcing blog posts

After a blog post merges, announce it across platforms:

Via issue comment:

/jinx announce https://rladies.org/blog/my-post/

What happens:

  1. announce_post() reads the post’s YAML front matter
  2. create_announcement_message() builds the message with hashtags
  3. Posts to Bluesky (post_bluesky()), Mastodon (post_mastodon()), LinkedIn (li_post_write()), and newsletter (send_newsletter())

Each platform is posted independently – failures on one do not block others.

Running reports

Activity reports

Via issue comment:

/jinx report weekly
/jinx report monthly

Programmatically:

report <- generate_report(type = "weekly")
publish_report(report)

Chapter health reports 

/jinx report chapters
url <- report_chapter_health()

Managing the GHA dashboard 

/jinx gha-dashboard

What happens:

  1. generate_gha_dashboard() queries workflow run status across all org repos
  2. publish_gha_dashboard() posts a formatted status table as a GitHub issue

Scheduled workflow runs weekly via gha-dashboard.yml.

Airtable sync

Scheduled workflow runs weekly via airtable-sync.yml.

Programmatically:

sync_directory_airtable()
sync_global_team_airtable()

Syncs directory entries and global team data between Airtable and GitHub. Creates PRs for any changes detected.

Slack onboarding 

send_slack_invites(dry_run = TRUE)

Fetches pending invitees from Airtable and sends Slack workspace invitations. Use dry_run = TRUE (the default) to preview before sending.

RSS feed integration:

subscribe_slack_rss()

Subscribes Slack channels to the R-Ladies blog RSS feed.

Directory maintenance

Validating directory PRs:

The validate_directory_pr() function runs automatically on PRs that modify directory entries. It checks:

  • YAML schema compliance
  • Filename conventions (validate_entry_filename())
  • Image optimization (crop_directory_image(), optimize_image())
  • Social handle verification (verify_social_handles())

Contributor recognition

List contributors for a repo:

/jinx contributors jinx

Update the contributors list:

/jinx contributors update jinx

Org-wide contributor stats:

/jinx contributors org

Automated greetings:

  • welcome_contributor() posts a welcome comment on first-time PRs
  • thank_contributor() posts a thank-you when PRs are merged

Both are triggered by the welcome-contributor.yml and thank-contributor.yml workflows.

PR review automation

PR review runs automatically on pull requests via reusable workflows.

What happens:

  1. review_pr() applies the rules from review-rules.yml
  2. Reviewers are assigned based on file paths and team ownership
  3. Labels are applied based on labels.yml mappings
  4. check_pr_naming() enforces branch and title conventions

See vignette("getting-started") for how to add PR review to other repos.

Stale issue reminders 

/jinx remind stale

gt_remind_stale() scans for onboarding/offboarding issues that have gone stale and posts reminder comments.

On this page
